items.title: Three pillars of the curse
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  curse["Curse of Dimensionality"]
  sparse["High-d sparsity\nfixed n spreads thin"]
  dist["Distance concentration\nall points look similarly far"]
  sample["Sample complexity\ncovering space needs r^d cells"]
  curse --> sparse
  curse --> dist
  curse --> sample
  sparse --> local["weak local neighborhoods"]
  dist --> knn["unstable k-NN rankings"]
  sample --> data["need exponentially more n"]
items.caption: Canonical phenomena owned by this topic: sparsity, distance concentration, and sample complexity drive practical ML failures in high d.

items.title: Volume vs dimension for fixed-radius neighborhood
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  lowd["low d: local ball covers meaningful volume"]
  highd["high d: same-radius ball is microscopic"]
  fixedn["fixed training count n"]
  fixedn --> lowd
  fixedn --> highd
  lowd --> good["informative neighbors"]
  highd --> bad["empty or unrepresentative neighbors"]
items.caption: High-dimensional sparsity: with fixed n, local regions contain fewer training points relative to total space.

items.title: Mitigation flow
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  problem["high d hurts distance-based learning"]
  reduce["reduce d: PCA, selection, embeddings"]
  better["better features: domain signal not noise columns"]
  model["change model: trees, linear with regularization"]
  more["collect more n if feasible"]
  problem --> reduce
  problem --> better
  problem --> model
  problem --> more
items.caption: Responses to the curse — algorithm details for PCA and k-NN live in linked topics.
